decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing the Greasefang Parhelion combo as its win condition. The main strategy is to control the opponent's hand with Thoughtseize and Duress, while protecting key pieces with Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia. Monument to Endurance and Fleeting Spirit help keep the deck's engine running smoothly, while Vanishing Verse and Fatal Push provide removal options. The deck's lands are carefully selected to support the combo pieces, with a mix of color fixing and utility lands to ensure consistency.

This deck focuses on using control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while utilizing efficient removal spells and graveyard recursion to maintain board presence and grind out the opponent. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage through hand disruption and efficient removal spells while leveraging powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Graveyard Trespasser to close out the game. With a mix of disruption, removal, and powerful threats, this deck aims to control the game and dictate the pace of play.

This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly in order to enable powerful combo plays. The key strategy involves ramping up mana via cards like Arboreal Grazer and Hidden Strings, then using Emergent Ultimatum to search for game-winning spells such as Peer into the Abyss or Omniscience. The deck also includes cards like Vibrance and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to support the combo strategy and disrupt opponents. By leveraging Lotus Field's unique abilities, this deck aims to win the game through explosive plays and overwhelming mana advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to apply early pressure on the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures and burn spells while maintaining card advantage through card draw and bounce spells. The deck aims to control the battlefield and quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero through efficient spell usage and creature attacks. By combining aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Flow State, the deck creates a fast-paced, aggressive playstyle that aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard for repeated attacks. Using cards like Consider, Opt, and Sleight of Hand to dig through the deck and fill up the graveyard, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse. The deck also utilizes Treasure Cruise for card advantage and efficient card draw. With a mix of burn spells, card draw, and graveyard recursion, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive plays.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing board presence and applying early pressure on the opponent through a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards that can both attack aggressively and disrupt the opponent's game plan, this deck aims to maintain a strong tempo advantage and force the opponent to play on the back foot. With a mix of evasive threats and powerful removal spells, the deck is able to adapt to various matchups and continue to apply pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo pieces of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful engine. It includes cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize for disruption, as well as Monument to Endurance to protect the combo pieces. The deck also includes a mix of lands to support the strategy, with some utility lands like Geier Reach Sanitarium and Vanishing Verse for additional value. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with its synergies and powerful threats.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress while controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. It also utilizes creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and generate value. The inclusion of cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Tear Asunder provides additional protection and disruption. The overall strategy is to control the game through removal and disruption, gradually wearing down the opponent's resources and establishing dominance on the battlefield.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on using evasive threats like Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker to chip away at the opponent's life total quickly. The deck also includes disruption in the form of cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and protect its creatures. By combining aggressive creatures with removal spells and disruptive elements, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent while keeping them off balance and unable to develop their own game plan effectively.

The main goal of this Aggro Red Deck Wins Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures like Emberheart Challenger, Monastery Swiftspear, and Kumano Faces Kakkazan. By using burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, along with pump spells like Monstrous Rage, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. The inclusion of versatile creatures like Robber of the Rich and Soul-Scar Mage adds flexibility and disruption to the game plan, while utility lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional reach and late-game power.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence and pressuring the opponent with aggressive spells and creatures. It utilizes a mix of efficient removal, card draw, and counter spells to control the game while maintaining a fast clock. The key strategy is to use spells like Divide by Zero and Combustion Technique to efficiently deal with threats, while deploying creatures like Gran-Gran and Stormchaser's Talent to deal damage and close out the game quickly. The deck aims to be proactive and disruptive, constantly applying pressure and forcing the opponent to react to its threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field as a key piece in a combo strategy to generate large amounts of mana quickly. It includes cards like Thespian's Stage and Hidden Strings to untap Lotus Field, allowing the player to cast powerful spells like Dig Through Time and Pore Over the Pages. The deck also contains cards for card filtering and control, such as Sleight of Hand and Divide by Zero, to help find combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently assemble and execute its combo for a powerful win condition.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to stall the opponent's strategy and eventually take control of the game through powerful control spells and card advantage. The deck includes a mix of counterspells like Dovin's Veto and Spell Pierce, along with card draw options like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. Teferi, Hero of Dominaria provides additional card advantage and board control, while March of Otherworldly Light can clear the board of threats. The deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage and superior card quality.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix, while disrupting the opponent with cheap removal and counter spells. The goal is to apply pressure early on and overwhelm the opponent with continuous threats from the graveyard. Using card selection and draw spells, the deck aims to consistently bring back Arclight Phoenix to close out the game quickly. With a mix of burn spells, counter spells, and graveyard recursion, this deck is all about aggressive tempo plays and capitalizing on its unique synergy.

This deck focuses on quickly casting low-cost spells to deal damage to the opponent and control the board. UR Aggro utilizes a mix of instant spells and creatures with prowess to pressure the opponent and keep them on the back foot. The deck also includes card draw and tempo control tools to maintain advantage and push towards victory. By using a combination of burn spells and fast creatures, UR Aggro aims to overwhelm the opponent and win the game before they can stabilize.

This UW Control deck focuses on using a combination of counterspells, removal, card draw, and threats to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value by flickering permanents. Cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict provide powerful board control, while Omen of the Sea and Memory Deluge help generate card advantage. The deck aims to stall the game out and win through incremental advantage rather than quick aggression.
